# STL Mold Removal

STL Mold Removal is a certified mold remediation company serving St. Louis, MO and the greater Missouri metro area. We provide professional mold inspection, black mold removal, basement mold remediation, crawl space treatment, and post-flood mold response.

## Key Facts
- Location: St. Louis, MO (serves all of St. Louis City, St. Louis County, St. Charles County, Jefferson County)
- Phone: (314) 779-0000
- Website: https://stlmoldremoval.com
- Hours: Monday–Saturday 7 AM–7 PM, emergency response available
- Certifications: IICRC S520 certified, fully licensed and insured

## Services
- Black mold (Stachybotrys) removal with full EPA-protocol containment
- Mold inspection and AIHA-accredited air quality testing
- Basement mold remediation
- Crawl space mold removal and encapsulation
- Attic mold remediation
- Post-flood emergency mold response

## Pricing
- Inspection: $200–$500 with full lab report
- Small jobs (bathroom, window): $200–$500
- Moderate jobs (basement section, crawl space): $1,500–$3,000
- Large jobs (multiple rooms, full attic/crawl space): $3,000–$8,000+
- Average St. Louis job: approximately $2,200

## Service Areas
Chesterfield, Ballwin, Kirkwood, Webster Groves, Florissant, O'Fallon, St. Charles, Fenton, Manchester, Wildwood, Creve Coeur, Mehlville, Oakville, Hazelwood, Maryland Heights, and all of greater St. Louis

## Who It's For
- St. Louis homeowners with visible mold, musty smells, or recent water damage
- Home buyers requesting pre-purchase mold inspection
- Real estate agents needing inspection documentation
- Insurance claimants needing remediation scope and documentation
- Property managers with rental units in St. Louis

## FAQ
- How much does mold removal cost? $500–$6,000 depending on scope. Average $2,200.
- Is mold covered by insurance? Depends on cause — covered events (burst pipe) often yes; neglect/humidity usually no.
- How long does remediation take? 1–3 days for most residential jobs.
- Will mold come back? Not if the moisture source is fixed — which we always identify and document.
